name: Spinach Enchiladas with Three Colors
recipeIngredient: 1 bunch Cilantro, chopped or 1 package Chives, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the stuffing: Combine cottage cheese and cream cheese in a food processor and blend until smooth. Add cumin powder, chili powder, and remaining ingredients, then stir into the cheese mixture.
2. Prepare Red Sauce: Wash dried chili anchos and remove seeds if desired (milder heat). Boil water, add chilies, and let soak. Melt butter in a frying pan, add onions and garlic, and sauté until clear. Set aside. Blend chilies and water until smooth, then stir into the butter mixture to form a roux. Add chili and liquid, creating a sauce. Adjust with water or tomato sauce if needed, simmering constantly until thickened.
3. Prepare Green Sauce: Simmer tomatillos in water until tender, blend, sauté onions and garlic, and mix with green sauce.
4. Dip tortillas in the red sauce while it is simmering to soften them. Fill each tortilla with a dollop of the cheese and spinach mixture, then roll seam side down into a greased glass casserole dish.
5. Cover enchiladas with red sauce.
6. Add dollops of sour cream on top for a vibrant red-green-white presentation that complements the Mexican flag. Garnish with chopped cilantro before serving.
